# Africa Global Forum

> A peer network for the African diaspora driven by one belief: we exist to create opportunity among ourselves — then with the world. Africans abroad help each other, sit together to share what they're going through, and bounce ideas.

Africa Global Forum (AGF) is led by Samuel Kimani Sikuku and operates at the corridor between Africa and its diaspora. We are a network — not a media brand and not a consultancy — and our work falls into three areas: peer help among diaspora members, community channels for those going through similar things, and live talks with open Q&A. AGF also runs affiliate partnerships with companies operating the Africa ⇄ Abroad corridor (past partners include Lemfi and Founders Running Club), and publishes free research on remittance, migration, capital and entrepreneurship.

## What we do

- **Help & Be Helped** — A diaspora marketplace to give, receive and pay it forward. Search the network for members who can help.
- **You Are Not Alone** — A dedicated channel to share what you're carrying and find others who carry it too. Working name: "We are together."
- **Bounce Ideas** — Inspiring talks from members, with an open Q&A floor afterwards.

## Reports

All Africa Global Forum reports are free to download.

- [African Footballers & Social-Media Fame — World Cup 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/african-footballers-world-cup-social-media-2026/): How World Cup moments are turning viral fame into digital millions for African footballers. The anchor case: Vozinha, the 40-year-old Cape Verde goalkeeper, going from ~50,000 to ~14M Instagram followers in 24 hours after a 0-0 draw with Spain on 15 June 2026 — a ~160× jump, with a single sponsored post projected at ~$68K (close to his entire on-field market value). Plus Morocco's 2022 precedent (Bono 1M→6.7M, Hakimi 21.7M, Ziyech 10.5M, Amrabat 5.1M), Mohamed Salah's 66M-strong empire built across three World Cups, Maduka Okoye going viral on TikTok without Nigeria even qualifying, the five-part virality formula, monetization pathways (sportswear deals, paid posts, image rights), and the diaspora dimension (CazéTV as a surrogate Lusophone diaspora amplifier). Full HTML article + 4-page PDF. ~15-min read.
- [African Migrants · Top Destinations — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/african-migrants-top-destinations-2026/): Where the 2 million Africans who emigrate every year actually go. Full top-10 destination profiles (France, USA, Saudi Arabia, UK, Germany, Canada, Italy, Spain, China, Australia) with key communities, pull factors and challenges. The structural pattern: colonial language corridors, salary multipliers, network effects. Full HTML article + 13-page PDF. ~18-min read.
- [Africa's Growing Economies — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/africa-growing-economies-2026/): 12 of the world's 20 fastest-growing economies are African. Profiles of all 12 (Ethiopia 9.2%, Guinea 8.7%, Uganda, Rwanda, Benin, Niger, Libya, Côte d'Ivoire, Djibouti, Tanzania, DR Congo, Mali) with key sectors, investor signals. Four structural forces: commodity-transition, demography, AfCFTA, digital economy. Plus debt/informality/IFFs challenges. Full HTML article + 12-page PDF. ~16-min read.
- [Remittance Apps in Africa — 2026 YTD](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/remittance-apps-africa-2026-ytd/): Mid-2026 snapshot of the apps moving money along the Africa ⇄ Abroad corridor. Market share of Wise, LemFi, NALA, Remitly, Sendwave, WorldRemit, Chipper Cash, MoneyGram. Fee structures, biggest movers YTD (UK→Nigeria, EUR→CFA, US→Kenya, Canada→Ghana, UAE→Ethiopia), regulatory shifts (CBN, BCEAO, FCA, FinCEN), stablecoin back-end layer. HTML page + 11-page PDF.
- [African Currency & Remittance Tracker — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/african-currency-remittance-tracker-2026/): 13-month tracker of top-10 African currencies vs USD/EUR (April 2025–April 2026). Winners (Ghana cedi -20.9%, ZAR -17.3%, Naira -9.8%) and losers (Ethiopia ETB +21.7%). App-by-app comparison of Wise vs LemFi vs NALA vs Remitly vs Western Union per $100 sent on five major corridors (USD→NGN, KES, GHS, ZAR; EUR→XOF). The three rules of smart remittances and best app per corridor. Full HTML article + 10-page PDF. ~14-min read.
- [Sending Money Home to Africa — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/sending-money-home-to-africa-2026.pdf): Fees, FX margins and what really reaches home — a fact-checked community guide for the diaspora. 8 pages.
- [Honest Questions and Answers from AI — Edition 02 · Work, Career & Studies (2026)](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/honest-questions-work-career-studies-2026/): Second edition of the AGF 'Honest Questions and Answers from AI' category. Five quiet questions every African abroad carries about their work and career, each put to a frontier AI and answered in the Fable-5 card format. (1) Earning respect at work in a Western country without changing your whole personality. (2) Any job now vs. waiting for a role closer to your qualifications. (3) Negotiating salary as the only African/foreigner in the room. (4) Local professional qualifications vs. portable online certifications. (5) Dealing with subtle racism and being underestimated without losing your job or your peace.
- [Honest Questions and Answers from AI — Edition 01 (2026)](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/honest-questions-sending-money-home-2026/): First edition of the AGF 'Honest Questions and Answers from AI' category — a new AGF format distinct from research reports. Five quiet questions every African-diaspora sender asks before opening the remittance app, each put to a frontier AI and answered in the Fable-5 'resolved to answer thoughtfully' card format. (1) Invest here vs. buy land back home? (2) Supporting parents without family dependency. (3) How much is reasonable to send monthly? (4) Avoiding debt while sending home. (5) Saying no without being seen as proud or selfish. (1) Should I prioritize investing where I live now, or buying land back home? — Start by securing financial roots where you are planted; build the safety net abroad first, then buy land home from a position of strength. (2) How do I support my parents without creating whole-family dependency? — Separate honoring your parents from sponsoring the extended family economy; define what you take responsibility for and design your budget around that. (3) How much is reasonable to send each month? — The reasonable amount is what remains after protecting your own basics and future; treat it like a bill you pay on time, not a guilt-driven variable. HTML page. ~5-min read.
- [Banks vs. Stablecoins — Europe to Africa 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/banks-vs-stablecoins-europe-africa-2026/): Fact-checked country-by-country comparison of bank-wire vs. USDC/USDT cost, speed and regulation for the Europe → Africa remittance corridor. Bank wires cost 8–12% and take 3–5 business days; stablecoins cost 1.5–4.5% and settle in 10–60 minutes. Deep-dive on Africa's top 10 economies (South Africa, Egypt, Algeria, Nigeria, Morocco, Kenya, Ethiopia, Angola, Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana). Regulatory split: SA/NG/KE/GH regulated; Algeria explicitly banned via Law 25-10 (July 2025); Egypt/Morocco/Ethiopia/Angola restricted or grey-zone. Nigeria received ~$92.1B in on-chain crypto value (Chainalysis 2025); SSA moved ~$205B on-chain +52% YoY. Key platforms (Coinbase/Kraken/Binance on the EU side under MiCA; Yellow Card/VALR/Kotani Pay/Luno/Busha on the African side). Practical recommendations per corridor, including 'do NOT use stablecoins in Algeria'. Full HTML article + 12-page PDF. ~18-min read.
- [Best Apps for Africans Abroad · By Region — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/best-apps-africans-abroad-by-region-2026/): The apps Africans abroad actually use — money (Wise, LemFi, NALA, Remitly, Chipper Cash), calling (WhatsApp, Botim), identity, banking (Revolut, Wise, Monzo, N26) — ranked by region (Europe, North America, Gulf, Asia). HTML page + 16-page PDF.
- [Best Countries for African Students — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/best-countries-african-students-2026/): Where to study, work and stay — ranked for African students looking abroad. Tuition costs, post-study work rights (Canada PGWP, UK Graduate Route, Germany job-seeker, Australia 485, France Talent Passport), immigration pathway, scholarships (DAAD, Commonwealth, Chevening, Fulbright, CSC, Vanier, Eiffel, MEXT), cost of living. Recommended pick by student profile (undergrad / master's / PhD / vocational). HTML page + 15-page PDF.
- [African Diaspora Entrepreneurs — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/african-diaspora-entrepreneurs-2026/): ~30 African diaspora unicorns in under 10 years. Profiles of 10 unicorn founders (Ismail Ahmed/WorldRemit, Aboyeji/Flutterwave+Andela, Awotona/Calendly, Wemimo/Esusu, Abuzeid/Incredible Health, Kent-Braham twins/Marshmallow, Udotong/Fireflies.ai, Belamant/Zilch, Serunjogi+Moujaled/Chipper Cash). Rising stars 2026 (Fernandes/NALA, Mbaabu/Cloud9, Ene-Obong/Syndicate Bio, Taboure/Anka, Procter/Pharos). Sector breakdown, how they raised capital, seven lessons. Full HTML article + 10-page PDF. ~15-min read.
- [Return Migration — Africa 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/return-migration-africa-2026/): Who is going home, why, and what the returning African diaspora is reshaping. Return drivers (family, opportunity, identity, policy, fatigue), top return destinations (Lagos, Nairobi, Accra, Kigali, Cairo, Cape Town, Casablanca), sectors being built by returnees (tech, healthcare, agriculture, finance, real estate, creative), dual citizenship and right-of-return policy frameworks across Ghana/Nigeria/Kenya/Rwanda. HTML page + 10-page PDF.
- [Navigating Cultural Identity — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/navigating-cultural-identity-2026/): For the Africans abroad holding two worlds — how the diaspora keeps, blends and rebuilds identity. Cross-cutting themes (culture as dynamic, identity defined internally, language/food/traditions as anchors, the 'third culture' experience), the integration debate, and a curated set of African-diaspora discussions on raising children with heritage language and pride. Full HTML article + 11-page PDF. ~15-min read.
- [Africa AI Landscape — 2026](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/africa-ai-landscape-2026/): Africa's AI market $4.5B (2025) → $16.5B (2030) at 27% annual growth. The Big Four (Kenya, Nigeria, South Africa, Egypt) take 83% of AI startup funding. Rising tier (Rwanda, Morocco, Ghana, Ethiopia, Senegal). Sector breakdown (fintech 28%, agriculture 22%, healthcare 18%, education 12%). Infrastructure reality (1% global AI compute, 3% AI talent, 140+ data centres). Homegrown ecosystem (Lelapa AI's InkubaLM, Masakhane, Kencorpus, minoHealth, Apollo Agriculture, Zindi). Big Tech bets ($1B Microsoft-G42 Kenya, $1B Google pan-Africa, NVIDIA Cassava AI Factory). Honest challenges: brain drain, jobs paradox, data sovereignty, regulatory fragmentation. Five diaspora opportunity tracks. Full HTML article + 10-page PDF. ~15-min read.
- [Blockchain & Cross-Border Africa — 2025](https://africaglobalforum.com/reports/blockchain-cross-border-africa-2025/): How on-chain rails are reshaping cross-border value across the continent and its diaspora. Stablecoin adoption (USDC, USDT), regulatory landscape (CBN, Mauritius VAITOS, South Africa FSCA, Kenya, AfCFTA), key projects (Yellow Card, Bitnob, Mara, VALR, Luno, Kotani Pay, Mansa, Yellow Pay), four diaspora use cases (remittance settlement, FX hedging, savings, dollar exposure), the on-chain back-end of off-chain remittance apps. HTML page + 8-page PDF (archive 2025).

## Founder

- Samuel Kimani Sikuku, Founder of Africa Global Forum. Based between Nairobi and London.

## Partners (Africa ⇄ Abroad corridor)

- Lemfi — fintech / remittance
- Founders Running Club — community

## Membership

Application-based. We read every application personally and write back within two weeks. Apply at https://form.typeform.com/to/zKmIFOmA — follow updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/98224182/

## Policies

- [Code of Conduct](https://africaglobalforum.com/code-of-conduct/) — values, expected member behaviour, the rules of the "You Are Not Alone" channel, how we handle problems.
- [Terms & Conditions](https://africaglobalforum.com/terms/) — what AGF is, how membership works, data and privacy practices, intellectual property of the research, partner disclosure, standard disclaimers.
